commands.review_summary:
The PR changes link-insights `today` range calculation from local midnight to UTC midnight and adds a regression test around the UTC boundary.

Reproducibility: yes. The current-main source path is clear, and a direct Node check of the same `setHours` logic shows the `today` boundary shifting to `2026-05-11T04:00:00.000Z` under `TZ=America/New_York`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining actions are contributor-supplied proof and maintainer confirmation of the `Today` boundary semantics, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(link-insights): anchor "today" range to UTC midnight to match created_at
target.body:
Anchor the link-insights "today" window to UTC midnight so it lines up with the UTC `created_at` data it queries.

## Problem
The link-insights `today` range drifts by the host machine's UTC offset, and the repo's own `src/lib/link-insights.test.ts` is timezone-flaky: green under `TZ=UTC`, red under `TZ=America/New_York` (and worse under far-offset zones). On a machine east or west of UTC, "today" silently drops or includes the wrong day's links.

## Root cause
`resolveRange` computes the start of "today" with `start.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0)`, which snaps to the host's **local** midnight. That `since` is then compared in SQL against `o.created_at`, which is stored as a **UTC** ISO string (`o.created_at >= ?`). Local midnight and UTC midnight differ by the host offset, so the window boundary lands on the wrong instant.

## Fix
One line: `start.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0)` becomes `start.setUTCHours(0, 0, 0, 0)`, so the boundary matches the UTC data the query reads. The week/month/year/all ranges use a rolling `setDate(... - days)` window and are unchanged.

## Scope note
The codebase computes "today" two different ways: link-insights (this function) versus the period-digest path, which uses local day boundaries (`localDateStart`) by design. This PR only makes link-insights self-consistent with the UTC data it queries and stops the timezone-flaky test. Whether to also make the digest UTC, or make everything local, is a separate design call and is intentionally left to the maintainer.

## Tests
- The existing link-insights tests are now timezone-stable instead of only passing under `TZ=UTC`.
- Added one timezone-independent regression test that pins the boundary: two occurrences straddle UTC midnight of the fixed `now` by 30 minutes on each side, and only the after-midnight one is classified as "today". This assertion holds under any host timezone only when the window starts at `00:00:00.000Z`, so the boundary cannot silently revert to local. It is RED before the fix (under any non-UTC zone) and GREEN after.

## Verification
- Full test file passes under `TZ=UTC`, `TZ=America/New_York`, `TZ=Pacific/Kiritimati` (UTC+14), and `TZ=Pacific/Honolulu` (UTC-10).
- `pnpm run check` (format + lint), `pnpm run typecheck`, and the full `pnpm test` suite (110 files, 1035 tests) all pass.

Found and fixed with the help of the Claude CLI.
target.author: devyrpauli
